steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The basking shark runs 1.0–1.5 °C above ambient (heat retention), yet its temperature still declines as ambient temperature falls. Despite generating metabolic heat, its body temperature tracks the environment.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — basking shark has a fixed body temperature: The data shows its temperature varies with ambient conditions, not fixed. ✗
  • Choice B — both species' temperatures are at least partly determined by ambient water: Pacific halibut is fully ectothermic; the basking shark's temperature still tracks ambient despite heat retention. Both fit. ✓
  • Choice C — basking shark is fully ectothermic like Pacific halibut: The basking shark does retain some heat (1.0–1.5 °C above ambient), so it is not fully ectothermic. ✗
  • Choice D — Pacific halibut can regulate its own temperature: No data in the passage supports this; halibut is described as ectothermic. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
